summary: Replacement client_engineer hardened M6 Alpha core-flow accessibility within allowed web/UI/test/process paths. The map read-model viewport now has keyboard navigation help, aria-keyshortcuts, aria-roledescription, visible focus, and a polite selected-district status. M6 scenario, command preview/confirm, command status, save/load checkpoint, policy/event choices, encyclopedia, diplomacy, succession, adviser, and terminal review surfaces expose screen-reader descriptions, text/list semantics, live status, and non-color data-status-kind evidence. React still consumes read-model slices and protocol command DTOs only; no full WorldState, sim-core rule change, telemetry, account, hidden network call, Electron IPC, production dependency, or manual node battle UI was introduced.

tests_run.result: After implementation, only allowed paths were modified: apps/web/src/shell/web-client-shell.tsx, packages/ui/src/index.tsx, tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts, tests/ui-m2-district-list.test.tsx, task JSON, and this REVIEW handoff.

1 test file passed; 8 tests passed, including M6 Alpha SSR checks for live status, screen-reader descriptions, policy choices, empty/error/extreme states, list semantics, and no WorldState text leakage.
Vite web build passed. Existing chunk-size warning was reported; no dependency or bundle-split change was made.
Storybook build completed successfully. Existing chunk-size warning was reported.

11 Chromium Playwright tests passed. M6 accessibility smoke covers keyboard focus order from map controls through map viewport, arrow-key district navigation, M6 scenario start, command preview/confirm, save/load checkpoint, live command/save status, non-color data-status-kind states, screen-reader label/description spot checks, reduced-motion transition clamp, and viewport overflow at 1280x720, 1280x800, 1920x1080, and 2560x1080.
Architecture boundaries, sim determinism, full root Vitest suite, and web package tests passed: 40 root test files / 305 tests plus web 1 file / 4 tests.

decisions: Kept all implementation in existing React DOM, CSS, read-model, and Playwright/Vitest patterns; no production dependency was added.
Exposed map keyboard navigation and selected district state through DOM semantics around the Pixi host while leaving Pixi command-style rendering and read-model authority boundaries intact.
Added M6 scenario, command, and save/load descriptions via aria-describedby so screen-reader spot checks can verify what each core-flow control does without duplicating domain rules in UI logic.
Kept M6 status evidence textual and attribute-based through role=status, aria-live=polite, aria-atomic, and data-status-kind values rather than relying on color alone.
Preserved DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE: the UI continues to state manual node battle is absent and no manual battle implementation, spike, or task-blocking surface was added.

Did not add .storybook stories because .storybook/** is outside this task's allowed paths. Existing Storybook build passes; normal/empty/error/extreme M6 state coverage is in Vitest and core-flow behavior is in Playwright.
risks: This is Alpha core-flow accessibility hardening only, not final M7/M8 accessibility completion, localization coverage, or platform accessibility certification.
Manual assistive-technology review was not performed in this implementation thread; evidence is automated role/name/description/live-region/focus/status spot checks plus recorded limits.
The Playwright focus-order smoke starts from the first map mode control because Chromium in this environment has not consistently focused the document from the first Tab after navigation.

Storybook state matrix remains limited by the allowed path list for this route; QA should treat the passing Storybook build and Vitest/E2E state coverage as this task's bounded evidence, not final complex-UI Storybook completeness.
requested_action: Independently review M6-ACCESSIBILITY-CORE-FLOWS-001 for Alpha core-flow accessibility evidence, keyboard/focus/live-region/non-color/viewport coverage, read-model and command/query boundary compliance, forbidden path compliance, DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE preservation, and recorded limits. Return ACCEPT, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK; do not mark ACCEPTED/CLOSED.
